content.verdict: The Retreat is the boutique 20-suite arrival/departure base for any well-planned Rwanda honeymoon — Africa's first 100% carbon-neutral hotel, run by Josh and Alissa Ruxin who founded Heaven Restaurant next door. Solar power, salt-water pool, organic kitchen garden, walking distance to the Genocide Memorial. The Kiyovu neighbourhood is residential and quiet; this is not a city-centre Marriott.
content.best_room: The Pool Suite has direct access to the salt-water pool deck and a private courtyard. The Junior Suites are excellent value with king beds and rainforest-style outdoor showers. The Honeymoon Suite (when available) has a four-poster bed and the largest tub.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival night before gorilla trek
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at KGL, 25 min to the hotel. Welcome dinner at Heaven Restaurant next door — Rwandan ingredients (cassava leaves, tilapia, isombe) cooked with international training. Early to bed for tomorrow's drive to Volcanoes.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Transfer to Volcanoes / Kinigi
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn breakfast. 2.5h drive to Volcanoes lodges (Bisate, One&Only, Sabyinyo, Singita). Hand off to your gorilla-trek lodge for the next 3-4 nights.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Volcanoes Park (at trek lodge)
itinerary_7_nights.description: First gorilla trek from your Volcanoes lodge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Volcanoes Park (at trek lodge)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Second gorilla trek + Dian Fossey grave hike.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Return to The Retreat in Kigali
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive back to Kigali (2.5h). Arrive by lunch. Afternoon at the salt-water pool, organic-garden tour, couples' massage in the spa. Dinner at Heaven — order the Rwandan tasting menu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Genocide Memorial + departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning visit to the Kigali Genocide Memorial (15 min walk) — challenging but essential. Lunch at the memorial gardens café. Afternoon at Inema Arts Center for Rwandan contemporary art. Evening departure flight from KGL.

caveats: Boutique 20-suite size — Wi-Fi can be patchy in some suites; ask for Pool Suite range.
Kigali altitude (1,500m) — generally comfortable but plan for cool evenings (15°C).
Heaven Restaurant is excellent but room service menu is limited; eat downstairs.
Carbon-neutral commitment means no air conditioning — fans only (climate makes this fine).
20-min walk to most other restaurants; Uber/Move is the easiest evening transport.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Excellent as the city bookend of a gorilla trip — boutique scale, romantic salt-water pool, and the meaningful Heaven Restaurant story. Not the destination itself, but the perfect punctuation.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Year-round — Kigali sits at altitude with mild climate (18–25°C). Avoid March–May rains for outdoor Memorial garden visits.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: 3–4 months for honeymoon dates; the Pool Suite books fastest.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, but boutique scale and quiet residential location make it adult-leaning in practice.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Pool Suite for the private deck; Junior Suite for value; Honeymoon Suite when available for the four-poster.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Kigali (KGL — direct from Brussels, Doha, Addis, Amsterdam, Nairobi). 25 min hotel transfer included.

content.email_template:
